title: Yellow Jacket Removal in Westbury, NY | $350-$550
metaDescription: Professional yellow jacket removal in Westbury, Long Island. Safe nest elimination for homes and businesses. Serving Westbury since 2012. Call now for fast service!
intro: Yellow jackets pose a serious threat to Westbury residents and businesses, especially during late summer when colonies are at peak size. Our expert technicians provide fast, safe yellow jacket removal throughout Westbury, from Old Westbury Gardens to the SUNY Old Westbury campus area. We eliminate nests completely while protecting your family, pets, and property.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has served Westbury homeowners and businesses for over a decade with licensed, insured technicians who understand Long Island's unique pest challenges. We offer same-day emergency service, use eco-friendly treatments safe for children and pets, and provide a satisfaction guarantee on all yellow jacket removals.
localContext: Westbury's tree-lined residential streets and commercial properties near the Miracle Mile provide ideal nesting sites for yellow jackets in wall voids, eaves, and landscaped areas. The area's mix of historic homes and newer developments requires customized removal approaches that protect architectural integrity while eliminating aggressive colonies.

faqs.question: How quickly can you remove a yellow jacket nest in Westbury?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day emergency service for Westbury residents. Most yellow jacket nest removals are completed within 1-2 hours, though larger or hidden nests may require follow-up treatments. We're typically on-site within 2-4 hours of your call during peak season.

faqs.question: What's included in the $350-$550 yellow jacket removal price?
faqs.answer: Our pricing includes full inspection, nest location and identification, complete nest elimination, treatment of the surrounding area to prevent returns, and a 30-day warranty. The final cost depends on nest size, location accessibility (ground vs. wall void vs. high eave), and the number of nests on your property.

faqs.question: Are yellow jackets dangerous to my family in Westbury?
faqs.answer: Yes, yellow jackets are aggressive defenders of their nests and can sting multiple times. Unlike bees, they don't lose their stinger and become more aggressive in late summer and fall. They're particularly dangerous for anyone with allergies, and disturbing a nest can result in dozens of stings. Professional removal is always recommended.

faqs.question: How do I know if I have yellow jackets vs. other wasps or bees?
faqs.answer: Yellow jackets have bright yellow and black stripes, are smaller than hornets, and build paper nests in protected areas like wall voids, underground, or eaves. They're scavengers often found around garbage and outdoor food. Our technicians will identify the species during inspection to ensure proper treatment methods are used.
